sections.title: Before Shield of Arrav is started

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Hello, stranger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there! The name's [player name]. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: My name is Reldo. I'm the palace librarian.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, that explains why you're in the library then.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Well, truth be told, I would probably be here even if it wasn't where I worked. I very much like reading, not to mention sharing what I learn with all who are interested.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a very noble goal!

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Hello there, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm in search of a quest.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Hmm... I don't believe there are any here.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Let me think actually...

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Ah, yes. I think I have something, if you're definitely interested?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Well, if you look around for book called The Shield of Arrav, you'll find a quest in there.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I'm not sure where the book is mind you... but I'm sure it's around here somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't think I am. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Wise Old Man of Draynor Village sent you this message.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Ah, I am always delighted to hear from him. You would not imagine the depths of his wisdom!

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I'd give you a reward, but you don't seem to have any space for it. Come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: King Roald has been very generous with my salary, so I can spare you some coins for your trouble.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: These little things seem to be everywhere these days! Perhaps you'd like them?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: My old friend Aubury sent me some runes in return for some books he wanted. Perhaps you'd like them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have anything to trade?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Only knowledge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much do you want for that then?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: No, sorry, that was just my little joke. I'm not the trading type.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I am the palace librarian.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah. That's why you're in the library then.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Although I would probably be in here even if I didn't work here. I like reading. Someday I hope to catalogue all of the information stored in these books so all may read it.

sections.title: Standard dialogue after Shield of Arrav

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Hello there, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have anything to trade?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Only knowledge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much do you want for that then?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: No, sorry, that was just my little joke. I'm not the trading type.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I am the palace librarian.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah. That's why you're in the library then.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Although I would probably be in here even if I didn't work here. I like reading. Someday I hope to catalogue all of the information stored in these books so all may read it.

sections.title: During The Knight's Sword or The Giant Dwarf

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Hello there, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about the Imcando dwarves?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: The Imcando dwarves, you say? Let's see...

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Ah, yes... For many hundreds of years, they were the world's most skilled smiths. They used secret smithing knowledge passed down from generation to generation.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Unfortunately, about a century ago, the once thriving group was wiped out during the barbarian invasions of that time.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I believe a few of them survived, but with the bulk of their population destroyed, their numbers dwindled even further. If any are left now, they'll no doubt keep to themselves.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: However, I have heard tales of their love for redberry pie. Perhaps one of those could convince one to reveal their true heritage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I just go around offering random dwarves redberry pie?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Well, not totally random. Apparently some of the surviving Imcando made a home for themselves near the cliffs on the Asgarnian southern peninsula. That would be a good place to start.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about this brooch?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I've never seen that symbol before. Where did you find it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In a cave beneath Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Very odd. Have you any idea how it got there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A goblin might have dropped it.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I've never heard of a goblin carrying a brooch like this. But just a minute...

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: The other day I filed a book about ancient goblin tribes. It's somewhere at the west end of the library, I think. Maybe that will be of some use.

sections.title: During A Tail of Two Cats

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Hello there, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a cat related question.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Meow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... My cat is trying to say something but I'm not wearing the Amulet of Catspeak.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Go on... ask him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay. I'm on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything about Robert the Strong?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Ah, Robert the Strong. There's some details on him in one of my books on the history of Gielinor. Let's see now...

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Fourth Age... Popular Lore... Ah here we are, Robert the Strong!

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: 'Not much is known about the hero called Robert the Strong. Most folk tales describe him as being taller than the tallest human and far stronger than the strongest warrior.'

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: 'He wields a six foot tall longbow and travels with his faithful panther, Odysseus. He is said to have had a personal crusade against the dragonkin...'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dragonkin?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I'm coming to that.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: 'The stories tell of the dragonkin being an intelligent race of bird-like creatures that walked rather than using their wings. They are said to be immortal, but cannot reproduce.'

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: 'Because of this, they became very afraid of death and shunned all other races.'

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: 'Some even say the dragonkin made corrupted versions of themselves for protection, what we now know as dragons. However, this has never been proven.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Doesn't sound very believable.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: There's no doubt that some elements of these tales have been overexaggerated for storytelling purposes, but you should be careful of outright dismissing them.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Robert the Strong is a name that often comes up in stories from the Fourth Age. There's no reason not to believe that these folk tales are based on events that actually happened.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you Reldo, you've been most... helpful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was a nice tale for mothers to tell their children, but I don't see how this helps Bob?

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Don't you think it's odd that no one knows where Bob came from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess, but he's just a cat... right?

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: I'd hoped you would've understood by now that there's more to cats than humans believe!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, ok! I get your point, after all I'm being dragged round Gielinor by... my cat!

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: I wouldn't say dragged... let's call it a partnership!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So are you saying that Robert the Strong is... is...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bob?!

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Let's ask Bob!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go!

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Have you found that book yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it here.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Wonderful, let's have a look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what does the book say?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Okay let's see... The Corvo family... Here we are! According to this there was a family by the name of Corvo living in Avarrocka for most of the Fourth Age.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: The last entry is from the year 1770 of the Fourth Age.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to them?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Impossible to say for sure but it was around that time that many people left Misthalin to settle in Morytania. It's possible that the Corvo family was among them.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I'm afraid that's all the help I can provide. Port Phasmatys is where most of the Morytania settlers wound up. Perhaps you'll find what you're looking for there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks Reldo.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found it but then I lost it.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: You lost it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry. I'm a terrible person.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Well you'd better find it again. These books are very valuable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I know it's around here somewhere. It's red if I remember right.

sections.title: Talking to him after showing him the Varrock census records

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Did you have any luck finding the Corvo family?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Port Phasmatys might be a good place to look. It's where most of the Morytania settlers wound up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I was wondering if you could help me with something.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: What do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for information on a Fourth Age hero. His name was Tristan and he fought alongside Robert the Strong.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Ah you must mean Tristan Corvo. I can't remember anyone ever asking about him before, most prefer to hear about the bigger heroes like Arrav or Robert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you know about Tristan?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Oh yes. What was it you wanted to learn?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to learn about his descendants. I know they lived in this area.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Well we do have census records which date back to when the city was still called Avarrocka. I'm afraid we don't have anything from before that time though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. It might still be worth a look. Where can I find this information?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I have a book around here somewhere with all of the details, why don't you see if you can find it. It's red if I remember right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Reldo, I have come to ask you to help with something.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: What can I do for you [player name]?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Ah! Do you know about hot and cold clues?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I do.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Well then use this strange device to feel where your destination is and dig.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: You should make some room for it before I can give you it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Ah, you use a strange device to tell you how far away you are from the place you need to be. When you get to your destination you dig.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Well then use this strange device to feel where your destination is and dig.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: You should make some room for it before I can give you it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Reldo, I have one of those clue scrolls again, do you have any advice?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: That strange device will guide you in the same way the key did. Feel it, but be careful as it is very powerful. Good luck.
